title: Ghee-Glazed Kenyan Milk Skewers
description: A Kenyan dish featuring fermented milk, ghee glaze, and a char-grilled flavor profile. This recipe is perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: Soak the cedar plank in water for at least 30 minutes.
In a blender, combine the whole milk, ghee, fermented milk powder, sesame oil, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Thread the milk mixture onto the soaked cedar plank, leaving a 1-inch gap between each thread.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat. Place the cedar plank on the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
While the milk skewers are cooking, smoke the grill with wood chips for 5 minutes.
Once the milk skewers are cooked, brush them with the ghee glaze (made by melting the ghee in a saucepan over low heat).
Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves and serve immediately.
